vendor_name: INTERNACIONAL DE CALZADO TEN PAC SA DE CV
notes: Footwear vendor concentrated at PEMEX entities (PEP 169M, Refinacion 73M). Won LP contracts at PEP with 100% single-bid rate 2005-2008 (109M on 4 contracts with zero competition), then transitioned to 100% DA at PEP 2012-2013 (60M). Pattern consistent with institutional capture: competitive tenders with no other bidders followed by direct awards. Disappeared after 2013. Total PEMEX exposure ~300M across subsidiaries.

vendor_name: AVANZADA TECNICA CONSTRUCTIVA SA DE CV
notes: Construction firm with extreme institutional concentration. ISSSTE: 4 contracts worth 192M (60% of total value), 100% single-bid on competitive tenders, 2013-2014. AICM: 5 contracts worth 54M, 80% single-bid, 2015-2017. SAT: 8 contracts worth 53M, 100% DA, 2011-2013. Only 21 contracts total but 319M value — very high per-contract amounts. Pattern: wins massive ISSSTE construction via LP with no competition, then captures AICM similarly. Construction sector where SB on LP is a strong capture signal.

vendor_name: TELETEC DE MEXICO SA DE CV
notes: Telecom company winning LP tenders with extreme single-bid rates at INBA (329M, 67% SB) and multiple state-level institutions (Tamaulipas 71M risk=1.0, Coahuila 66M 100% SB, Yucatan 17M, Morelos 14M — all 100% SB). Peak activity 2008-2010 with 36-86% SB rates. 95% of 687M total came through LP yet 37% overall were single-bid. State obras publicas contracts from a telecom firm suggest potential industry mismatch. Pattern consistent with tender manipulation: LP filed but structured so no other vendor can bid.

Cleaning services vendor with 68% direct award rate and rapid value growth. AICM: 4 contracts worth 181M (38% of value), 100% DA, 2019-2021. ISSSTE: 9 contracts worth 142M (30%), 100% DA, 2018-2019. Combined AICM+ISSSTE = 68% of total 479M value, both at 100% DA. Only 41 contracts over 5 active years (2017-2021), but very high per-contract value. Classic P6 institutional capture pattern for cleaning services: sustained DA at major federal institutions.
